answer: The Amazon River is recognized as 6,400 kilometers long, but some reports suggest it may extend up to 7,062 kilometers. It is traditionally considered the second-longest river globally. As of 2026, the exact length remains debated.

results.title: Amazon River
results.snippet: It has a recognized length of 6,400 kilometers (4,000 mi), but according to some reports, its length varies from 6,575–7,062 kilometers (4,086–4,388 mi).
